title: State of Madras v. Champakam Dorairajan
summary: Supreme Court struck down Madras Communal G.O. fixing seats in state educational institutions based on religion and caste. It was the first case where Supreme Court struck down a law for violating fundamental rights. Led directly to First Constitutional Amendment adding Article 15(4) allowing reservations.
key_holdings: Communal G.O. reserving seats by caste/religion violates Article 15(1) and 29(2)
Directive Principles (Article 46) cannot override Fundamental Rights
First Constitution Amendment then added Article 15(4) to enable reservations
why_important: First case striking down law for violating FRs; directly caused First Constitutional Amendment adding positive discrimination clause.
relevance_to_legal_finance_system: Reservation law impacts HR hiring costs, compliance obligations, and government contract eligibility.
